One night while watching TV my husband and I jumped up because we were alarmed to smell burning plastic. We didn't know it was this heater at the time, and we tried narrowing it down but couldn't be sure if it was this heater or another (different) one we had on in the living room. A while later the smell went away.

After that I would smell it every now and then for the next couple of days, and I suspected this heater but couldn't be positive.

Today I smelled the heater again and thought I could hear a small noise coming from the heater but I didn't think much of it. I thought that something like dust might've gotten flown in there from sweeping all the time and that it'd go away soon. But then I heard more of an electrical "zap" noise and saw a huge spark come out the side of the heater. Turned that sucker off pretty quick after that.

My husband wanted to try to turn it on again to see what would happen and like other reviewers have said, it is now just blowing cold air out of it. The heater part must've shorted out.

I hope we can get our money back after only having it for two months, but I'm doubtful since I don't think we kept the receipt. If anything else noteable happens about returning this heater to the company, I'll add it here.

Update: The company wouldn't take the heater back since I didn't buy it from them, and where we bought it from wouldn't give our money back for it without a receipt. Instead they gave us a replacement. When this one breaks, I'm not really sure what we're going to do. I'm watching it like a hawk and so far it's been a month. If/when this one breaks too, I'll add to the review.

Update a week or two after the previous update, 1/7/2012: Well, it died. SURPRISE! Not. It didn't short out like last time though. This time when I went to change it from low heat to high heat, it simply made the little red light above the power buttong and high heat button stay on (sort of....they were more like wavering red lights) while the rest of it seemed to lose power. I unplugged it for a few minutes and plugged it back in since that's really all you can do to troubleshoot these things, but it stayed the same with the two little red lights and no other power.
I don't understand this since it's not even like we used a bad extension cord. The instructions say to plug it into the wall, but the cord is too short for that so I got a special extension cord specifically made for appliances like these heaters with certain wattage/voltage requirements, so we wouldn't short it out. But just like last time it didn't last. I suppose the company would say it's our fault for not plugging it directly into the wall or something, but it's not like we didn't go out of our way to help the short cord situation. And you should be able to use an extension cord with these heaters if you do your research (like for fans or vacuums or ANYTHING). There is no reason why it should just quit working except that it's junk. We're going to try to return it to the store AGAIN with no receipt and hope for store credit, but I'm not really getting my hopes up. Probably just got screwed.

Disclaimer: this is my first smart phone, so there is a learning curve. That said, I'm not a particularly picky person when it comes to this kind of stuff, so it doesn't take much to please me.

The phone is alright. My biggest peeve is that it doesn't have a shred of memory so you have to buy a mirco sd card to go with it before you plan on doing much other than making calls and texts and stuff. I should've probably known that before I bought it, but it's my first smart phone so what do I know?

My bad issues with my phone are basically due to the seller, Joycells. On different parts of the phone, like "bundles" and "channels" to choose from in the news feed area, or "device help", are all in spanish and either mexico- or argentina-based. As far as I've tried I've been unable to erase the mexican channels and bundles.
What made me most angry was that in the phone you can see the approximate data usage for the life of the phone. It said something like 13.65MB of data used since JUNE 18, 2011! So obviously this is a refurbished phone, not brand new.

I had a lot of trouble finding this phone and was really looking forward to it. I'm not very satisfied but I decided to stick with the stupid thing rather than return it just because I need a phone and it was a huge pain just to set it up and get service on it (didn't want to have to go through all of that again!). Since Joycells is an Amazon-approved seller, they gave me 20% of my money back and let me keep the phone rather than sending it back for 100% of my money back. Not sure if this was a wise choice or not.

Update 05/04/2014: So it turns out this phone really can't do much. Again, I should have known this by looking at the specs but seeing as how it was my first "smartphone," I had no clue. It is only Android 2.1 with no hope of updating it to anything more recent, so most apps aren't compatible with it. Apps such as most games, the Amazon app, Instagram, etc... so that's a huge disappointment.

On top of that--and I'm not sure, this might be just a STRAIGHT TALK issue--I am unable to receive mms (picture) messages. I can send them, but I can't receive them. I've tried EVERYTHING. I'm assuming this is Straight Talk's issue, so don't get this phone if you want to use them. Even their Straight Talk apps from the google app store are incompatible with it.

Overall, I'm pretty unhappy with this phone. It's about as useful as a year 2000 blackberry, or so it seems. I can get email and texts, and I can play a few select cheap games, but that's about it. I'll probably stick with it for 6 months or a year and then get something else. Which is a shame, because it's really cute and I get a lot of compliments on it. But for a "smartphone" it's really not that smart at all!

I bought this because of a home odor issue that I'm not sure of, since it involves a musty smelling storage room or maybe pet odor and I suspect some damp by the wall with picture window and I had no luck with anything else (other products just perfumed the air.)  I went back and forth between 2-3 stars because the use of this product caused extreme finger and hand pain. I sprayed one small room, a medium room and a tiny storage room.  How long could I have sprayed?  There is still product left in can.  I had hand pain for 24 hours, 36 hours later I can barely use my finger and I have a massive blister. Apparently this can was designed for people with very strong hands.  So the good was the spray didn't smell bad and disappeared in 15 minutes.  The house does smell better, how long it lasts I can't yet say.  The musty storage only improved slightly, but that was better than anything else I tried. If my finger ever heals, I might repeat spray the storage area. It's much too expensive for all these issues.  They did send it out very quickly and I received it on the expected delivery date.  I feel the directions for usage were poor and a bit confusing.  If I used it wrongly, I blame those directions on the can.

This is relatively small in size.  I bought it to create a little hospital for my houseplants when they start to look bad after not getting enough sun. The light coming from this lamp is a strange red/purple/blue coloration.  I don't know much about indoor plant lighting, but it seems ineffective.  Perhaps someone with a very specific need could use this crazy lamp, but for me it is just a nightlight now.  By the way, my plants don't seem to respond to it after a couple days under this light.

I will be returning this faucet because it was not a fit for my sink.  I understand that is my mistake and it has no bearing on the rating.  I only mention this so that others might double-check the measurements of their own sinks.  The finish of the oiled bronze faucet is pretty much black.  There is minimal to no bronze/copper metal shining through.  It has a slight matte appearance to it, and I can tell if you chose to, you could ‘wear down’ the finish to expose more metal underneath.  The faucet is heavy and well made, but did not come with any parts for the drain assembly.  I could purchase the oiled bronze drain from another retailer, but then it might not match this faucet.  I think if you’re selling a faucet, you need to go ahead and include the drain so that it is a matching set.  It’s great that they include the hot and cold water lines, but these are not likely to be needed if you are replacing an existing faucet.  Overall, it’s a good deal if you’re looking for a black faucet.

I've only had this for a few days, but I can't say I'm terribly impressed with it.

I've only managed to connect it to my phone once. I get a notification saying my band is recording my movements, but the app still tells me that I'm disconnected. So I click it to connect it and nothing shows up to connect it. The band itself will tell me I'm connected, but nothing shows up in the app. The one time I was able to connect it made my phone run hot and slowed it down. So the app is pretty useless. It doesn't store history, so you'd need to connect to the app every day to keep that information.

On the band itself, I wish I could pick and choose what functions to display. I do not need blood pressure or running (is this the GPS function?) or even heart rate. All I really wanted this for was for steps and distance. But you can't pick and choose what pages to flip through on the band. At least steps and distance are the first two.

I'm not really sure how accurate this thing is. I've only tested my resting heart rate twice, and I think it might be reasonable but I haven't tried it with activity yet. As for the blood pressure, I was sitting in the car yesterday and it told me my blood pressure was 151/101! I mean, grocery shopping can be annoying but I've never been that stressed about it. I've never had high blood pressure in my life. Why this is even on the wristband at all is a mystery to me since it'd be obviously very inaccurate considering how blood pressure is supposed to be measured.

I wore it to bed last night for the first time and it says I got 8.4 hours of sleep. Ok, I'll buy that I guess. But when I got up this morning it also said that I had walked 240 steps already. Unless I'm sleep walking--which I don't believe I do--that's a lot of extra steps for no reason.

I have it set for the wristband to turn on whenever I turn my wrist to look at it, but this function never works. I have to flick my wrist out and back in again, and sometimes that works but most of the time it doesn't. Usually I have to touch it to turn it on, which is fine, but it makes you wonder how accurate it really can be for anything if it can't even sense a wrist-flicking movement.

Lastly, I don't like wearing it to bed--but that's personal preference. Something bulky and hard like that on my wrist bothers me at night so I'm not going to bother with the sleep tracker. But when you take it off and lay it on a nightstand or whatever, a bright green light likes to shine and blink periodically. BRIGHT. It's from the underside where it would meet your wrist. So you'll need to cover it or stuff it in a drawer if that bothers you.

Overall I wouldn't recommend it. Look for something better instead of spending $30 on this thing.
